cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV should show end-to-end ownership of production platforms, not just people leadership. It needs concrete evidence of scaling a team, setting architecture, and keeping reliability and delivery under control while working with founders or other stakeholders. The best version also shows hiring and coaching examples, because this role explicitly asks for team growth.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: direct telecom industry leadership if not already proven
ownership of billing systems or telecom operators
GCP hands-on depth if it is not real
explicit management of a 5-15 person team if that scale was not actually reached
compliance ownership for regulated telecom environments
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Commander-4 is the closest match: it demonstrates platform ownership, many integrations, evaluation, guardrails, and production usage at scale.
Lantern maps well to reliability and routing decisions because it shows multi-provider routing and circuit-breaker style resilience.
The earlier CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices work supports the systems-thinking and architecture depth this role needs.

cv_translation.what_strong_cv_proves: A strong CV needs proof of building and operating live systems that can absorb growth while staying reliable. It should show leadership across architecture, delivery, and team development, plus concrete evidence that the candidate can work in constraint-heavy environments with real operational trade-offs. The best version also shows telco or telco-adjacent integrations explicitly, because that is a stated requirement here.
unsafe_claims_for_candidate: proven telecom industry experience if that has not been done
ownership of usage-based billing in telco if not real
carrier, MVNO, or CPaaS integrations if absent
GCP expertise if it was not hands-on
managing a larger engineering org than was actually led
best_candidate_experience_mapping: Lantern is the closest analogue to the telco integration and reliability problem because it shows multi-provider routing, failover, and operational safeguards.
Commander-4 demonstrates operating a live platform with many plugins, governance, and real users, which maps to the live-customer scale requirement.
The microservices, RabbitMQ, and real-time/WebRTC background supports the complex, data-heavy systems angle, but it does not substitute for explicit telecom experience.

It should also show enough hands-on technical depth to stay close to the product while building alignment, coaching people, and improving how an organization works. Because this role is in PHP and JavaScript, the CV would need real web-product examples in those languages to be convincing.

direct e-commerce marketplace ownership if not present
managing up to five product teams if that structure was not actually led

The CQRS/event-sourcing and microservices work is the best technical analogue for scalable web applications.
Lantern supports the leadership narrative around reliability and production ownership, but the CV will still need to bridge a noticeable PHP/JavaScript and e-commerce gap.
